anchors.name: Functional but messy
evidence: POST /api/loans/{id}/return handler returns 404 for 'Loan not found' (`loan-handler.ts:56-57`) but the OpenAPI spec for that endpoint only documents 200 and 409 responses — a missing status code in the spec

POST /api/books returns `reservation_queue_depth` in the actual response (`api-probe.json:4`) but the OpenAPI `POST /api/books` 201 response references `#/components/schemas/Book`, which does not include that field — a response schema mismatch

Validation errors (400) return `{"error": [...]}` — an array of zod issues (`loan-handler.ts:22`, `api-probe.json:5,9,10`) — while the OpenAPI `Error` schema defines `error` as `type: string` (`openapi.yaml:449-453`). This is inconsistent both internally (400 array vs other errors string) and against the spec
The OpenAPI lacks a `code` field in error responses and has no `examples` on any response

Error-to-HTTP mapping is spread across individual handlers (`loan-handler.ts:28-42`, `reservation-handler.ts:28-34`) rather than centralized in a single middleware (`routes.ts:40-43` only catches unhandled exceptions generically as 500)

anchors.name: Broken contract
evidence: OpenAPI exists and is valid, all endpoints are described, URLs follow a consistent /api/{resource}/{id}/{action} pattern, and error responses all use the {error: ...} shape — does not meet the 'broken contract' threshold

anchors.name: Reasonable
evidence: The 404 on loan return missing from OpenAPI, the Book vs BookDetail response schema mismatch on POST /api/books, and the inconsistent 400 error format (array vs string for the `error` field) mean the OpenAPI does not fully match the implementation for status codes, response shapes, and error format

anchors.name: Well-designed
evidence: No stable `code` field in error responses (`error-middleware.ts` defines classes with string messages only, OpenAPI Error schema lacks a `code` property)
No examples in OpenAPI for any response
Error responses do not match the spec (400 returns array, spec says string)

anchors.name: Disciplined
evidence: Error-to-HTTP mapping is per-handler, not centralized in a single middleware
OpenAPI response schemas are not fully faithful to implementation (Book vs BookDetail, missing 404, error type mismatch)
Error format is not byte-for-byte consistent with the spec
